title: gcc throw "lvalue required as left operand of assignment" when compiling (buggy) verilated code.
body:
A compile time error is generated from g++ when compiling `https://github.com/pulp-platform/rv_tracer.git` (with traces enabled).

I attached a minimal example derived from `rv_tracer` that retains the original structure. Below is the minimal example that can trigger the issue:

```
//
// Minimal trigger:
//
module dest_slice_too_large();
logic [31:0] prdata_o;
assign prdata_o[63:0] = 32'b0;
endmodule
```

The error in `rv_tracer` code is:
* APB bus is 32 bit
* Assignments to the data use: `prdata_o[XLEN-1:0] = ...`
With XLEN equal to 64, 3 warnings are produced:

```
      %Warning-SELRANGE: dest_slice_too_large.sv:32:25: Extracting 64 bits from only 32 bit number
         32 |                 prdata_o[te_pkg::XLEN-1:0] = upper_cause_q;
      %Warning-SELRANGE: dest_slice_too_large.sv:32:25: Selection index out of range: 63:0 outside 31:0
         32 |                 prdata_o[te_pkg::XLEN-1:0] = upper_cause_q;
      %Warning-WIDTHEXPAND: dest_slice_too_large.sv:32:44: Operator ASSIGN expects 64 bits on the Assign RHS, but Assign RHS's VARREF 'upper_cause_q' generates 32 bits.
```

In this case I would expect the second SELRANGE warning would be an error.
Minimum expectation would be that the generated code does not crash or throw compile-time errors

[dest_slice_too_large.sv.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23017895/dest_slice_too_large.sv.txt)

.

# What output from that test indicates it is wrong

A compile time error is generated with trace is enabled.
```
    Vdest_slice_too_large___024root__DepSet_hac016e96__0__Slow.cpp:31:5: error: lvalue required as left operand of assignment
       31 |     (QData)((IData)(vlSelfRef.dest_slice_too_large__DOT__prdata_o)) = 0ULL;
          |     ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
    make: *** [<>/verilator-5040/verilator/include/verilated.mk:272: Vdest_slice_too_large__ALL.o] Error 1
```

# What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?

```
verilator -cc -binary -fno-expand -Wno-fatal --trace-fst dest_slice_too_large.sv
```

# What 'verilator --version' are you using?  

Verilator is somewhat recent, build from git. 

```
Versions:
    gcc (GCC) 15.2.1 20250808 (Red Hat 15.2.1-1)
    Verilator 5.040 2025-08-30 rev v5.040
```
